records.ety: [L. consternatio, fr. consternare to overome, perplex, an accessory form of consternere to throw down, prostrate; con + sternere to spread out, throw down: cf. F. consternation. See Stratum.]

defs: Amazement or horror that confounds the faculties, and incapacitates for reflection; terror, combined with amazement; dismay.
quotes: The chiefs around, In silence wrapped, in consternation drowned. Attend the stern reply.
